description:
The always-on worker process. Same container image as the web tier, different entrypoint. Runs SQS consumer + cron-style jobs.

**Scope**
- `workers/src/index.ts` — multiplexes between SQS consumer mode and scheduled-job mode based on `WORKER_ROLE` env var.
- ECS Fargate task definition: 1 always-on `webhook-processor` task, additional tasks per scheduled job.
- EventBridge Scheduler rules → SQS messages that trigger handlers. Scheduled jobs registered here (full implementations live in their owning tickets):
  - `mdi-token-prewarm` (every 30 min) — refresh token if TTL <2 min.
  - `reconcile-stripe-mdi` (every 6h) — drift check (hardening phase).
  - `reconcile-kyc-pending` (hourly) — re-poll Persona for pending verifications >24h.
  - `audit-chain-verify` (nightly) — chain integrity (hardening phase).
  - `dunning-sync` (daily) — Stripe past_due → notify + pause MDI subscription.
- Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM → finish current message, drain SQS receive loop, exit clean. ECS deregisters via `stopTimeout`.
- Health endpoint at `:8080/health` for ECS health check.

**Acceptance**
- Killing a worker task mid-message does not lose the message (re-delivered after visibility timeout).
- All scheduled jobs are registered in EventBridge and visible in the AWS console.
- A bad image rollout doesn't take down running messages (blue/green at the ECS service level).
title: Background worker service (ECS Fargate + EventBridge schedules)
